Wanted to give a big shout out to @Sam Varón, @Steph Schaller, @Michelle Vega,  @Anna Trenary & @Kristin Turner (and more) for the awesome work being done on Sally Beauty :sally-beauty:. Today they had their Q3 earnings call in which the CEO called out directly how the personalization efforts were driving revenue growth for the entire organization. In fact, the word "Personalization" was mentioned SIX times during the earnings call.

From CEO: _"The combination of marketplaces, licensed colors on demand, and innovation, as well as personalization and enhanced performance marketing contributed approximately two ninety basis points of comp sales growth in the third quarter and two fifty points year to date"_

From CFO: "_Yeah, on the gross margin front in terms of Sally, very pleased with the performance there. I’m getting the planned and track the benefits  our field for growth effort in terms of how we’re redeploying that to drive that consistent traffic. It’s really our marketing efforts, CRM, our personalization, and then bringing innovation. So feel very good about continuing to maintain that profile as well as the traffic driving activities that we have that we’re deploying going forward."_

Excited for what this team can continue to accomplish as we finish out the FY and move into FY26 starting in October. Oh, and paired with the earnings call was a nice bump to their stock price as well :chart_with_upwards_trend:. Read the whole transcript here: <https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-sally-beauty-q3-2025-beats-eps-forecast-stock-surges-93CH-4170774|Earnings Call Transcript :taco:

Tacos for @Brian Davis and @Gressi Lopez for their work automating the WBR (Weekly Business Review) report for :intuit: CS! When we were introduced to this report it lived in a google sheet with I'll estimate somewhere between 20-40 people jumping in and making edits every week with very little process around it to manage changes (regularly adding new metrics) or enforce data governance (hint: accidental edits to calculations) and using only one live doc (no working vs final copy) - it was easy to understand why this report was a pain point for the Service Analytics team, especially since it has a lot of leadership visibility.

Enter Brian and Gressi.

Brian's organization around this report has been a lifesaver - keeping up with all the changes still going on in their google sheet report, pulling necessary updates into the Qlik dashboard and finding creative solves in Qlik for metrics with unique data source challenges :galaxy-brain:

And Gressi jumped on this project and picked up data source details from Jess to create a unified data source and got a v1 of this Qlik dash built out EARLY even with limited experience with Qlik prior to this project :qlik-cowboy:

We used to hear about our stakeholder for this report working weekends trying to manage all the changes going on in this dash, but today we heard how much he's actually getting to enjoy his weekends and relax and he's really excited for our dashboard review this week. Thank you so much for your work to give this report a full makeover! :taco:

I’ve written before about our participation in the Google Enhanced Conversions program, the brands it exposes us to and the work it has generated for the Analytics Development team.  Most of our engagements there are straightforward, new implementations.  Sometimes, however, we are called in to debug troubled sites that client, Google, and other teams have not been able to resolve.  The Disney engagement  this past spring is an example of that, and it helped cast Concord as the people who can fix the weird stuff.

A more recent example is Banco Itau, a Brazilian bank that had been struggling - along with Google - to understand why their EC data was not getting sent.  The debugging sessions were made more challenging by the language barrier, with the Google reps providing the English<->Portuguese translations.  @John Hartert worked with the invovled parties, looking “outside the square” at possible problems, helping the team to a resolution.

The following message was shared by Banco Itau’s account rep with the Google team that coordinates our work with them.  When you read the word “Concord,” substitute “John Hartert”:

_“Thank you for the connection with Concord - that was a big help for us indeed! We could collect evidence with them to understand the issue with Itau. Corrections are now in progress and we are properly implementing EC on Itaú!”_

Way to go, John - although I am certain this will lead to getting referrals to even weirder problems to solve!  :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

I was part of a QA Summit today and after listening to Steven Knopf I felt so inspired to share. I often struggle to convey the true benefits of having a Quality Engineer on a project. I often feel like people think it is just my “ways of working” and not a normal part of the role of a QE. Steven broke it out so well when he discussed the “Six Other Stances of a QE”.

1. Testing Mentor: use your expertise to help guide the whole team in better understanding proper testing
2. Quality Coach: continuously ask probing questions around impact and improvements
3. Engineer: understand the technology and architecture to be able to contribute and suggest strategies
4. Lifecycle Cultivator: advise on release strategies and when to shift left or right
5. Systems Thinker: have a wider view of the project, understand systems end to end
6. Voice of the Customer: (my favorite) not just testing but representing the user in testing and ensure the client’s project is meeting expectations
Just wanted to share my passion for all things processes and testing, and the value QE brings to a team.
Here is the link to Steven's LinkedIn if you would like to follow and learn more: <https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevenknopf/?originalSubdomain=uk

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!” 
• :handshake: Google sellers specifically request Concord to support their clients based on past experiences working with our team. 
• :construction_worker: Concord has become the go-to partner for advertisers who’ve tried to go it alone or have worked with other partners without success. This team has proven time and again that we can get it done.

Wanted to give another big shoutout to the Sally Beauty team on another successful quarter @Sam Varón @Steph Schaller @Rashmi Sandilya @Vaishnava @Liz King @Nick Bade @Kristin Turner @Anna Trenary (too many people to give tacos to). Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

Hey all -- campaign update here (and unfortunately it's not a super exciting one). I've made the decision that the current arc (Dal Boras) will be, at least for the foreseeable future, the final one that we run in this campaign. It's been a good run (over a year :exploding_head:) for the Wildguard and I'm really sad to see it come to a close. It wasn't a decision made lightly. We will continue through the Dal Boras arc -- however many sessions that takes.

Now, this doesn't mean that we stop D&D altogether -- if someone else wants to hop in the drivers seat and run a short campaign or one-shot, you should absolutely do that! I'm happy to help and share how I write, plan, and prep sessions for anyone that wants to learn. Otherwise, we can just do one-shots whenever it's convenient and works with people's schedules. Thanks for all of the effort that you guys have put in over the last year -- the thought and loving care that went into writing character backstories, roleplaying and decision-making at the table, and dedication to the campaign and each other did not go unnoticed. With that, I'm going to go cry in the bathroom.

Oh, also it looks like Tuesday is our day this week so be ready to roll at 6:30PM sharp!

This is late coming but a big ol’ kudos to @Josh Stephens for detecting and diagnosing underlying issues in key KPI used by ICS.   Josh noticed that aggregated tables were reporting larger denominators than the reported handled count for model based  metrics (i.e., imputed metric scores).  He went back to the fact table and found that rather than being dropped, null model scores were being assigned as negative interactions, depressing the derived metrics scores.  Way to go in improving Intuit’s ability to make informed decisions off their data.

This project was a huge lift over the past year, and the team delivered some foundational tech that’s going to set RT up for long-term success. A few highlights:
• A new event-based architecture with all MuleSoft APIs re-designed for scalability
• A full SDLC process with templates to bring consistency across projects
• The launch of LENS, a centralized logging dashboard giving RT visibility across all integrations (a first!)
• Strengthened QA processes, including performance testing and a path toward future automation
• (In flight) A new Unity Catalog built in Databricks to organize and govern data access more effectively

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!” 
• :handshake: Google sellers specifically request Concord to support their clients based on past experiences working with our team. 
• :construction_worker: Concord has become the go-to partner for advertisers who’ve tried to go it alone or have worked with other partners without success. This team has proven time and again that we can get it done.

Wanted to give another big shoutout to the Sally Beauty team on another successful quarter @Sam Varón @Steph Schaller @Rashmi Sandilya @Vaishnava @Liz King @Nick Bade @Kristin Turner @Anna Trenary (too many people to give tacos to). Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!” 
• :handshake: Google sellers specifically request Concord to support their clients based on past experiences working with our team. 
• :construction_worker: Concord has become the go-to partner for advertisers who’ve tried to go it alone or have worked with other partners without success. This team has proven time and again that we can get it done.

Enter Brian and Gressi.

Brian's organization around this report has been a lifesaver - keeping up with all the changes still going on in their google sheet report, pulling necessary updates into the Qlik dashboard and finding creative solves in Qlik for metrics with unique data source challenges :galaxy-brain:

Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!”

I’ve written before about our participation in the Google Enhanced Conversions program, the brands it exposes us to and the work it has generated for the Analytics Development team.  Most of our engagements there are straightforward, new implementations.  Sometimes, however, we are called in to debug troubled sites that client, Google, and other teams have not been able to resolve.  The Disney engagement from this past spring is an example of that, and it helped cast Concord as the people who can fix the weird stuff.

A more recent example is Banco Itau, a Brazilian bank that had been struggling - along with Google - to understand why their EC data was not getting sent.  The debugging sessions were made more challenging by the language barrier, with the Google reps providing the English<->Portuguese translations.  @John Hartert worked with the invovled parties, looking “outside the square” at possible problems, helping the team to a resolution.

The following message was shared by Banco Itau’s account rep with the Google team that coordinates our work with them.  When you read the word “Concord,” substitute “John Hartert”:

_“Thank you for the connection with Concord - that was a big help for us indeed! We could collect evidence with them to understand the issue with Itau. Corrections are now in progress and we are properly implementing EC on Itaú!”_

Way to go, John - although I am certain this will lead to getting referrals to even weirder problems to solve!  :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco: :taco:

• A total of $92M in spend efficiencies as a direct result of the work that we did to tune their MMM
    ◦ $76M of this was in streamlining paid media data and standardizing analysis cuts across channels
    ◦ $16M of this was in PPC spend optimizations by cutting the data hourly instead of daily to allow for more granular bidding strategies
• 35% increase in users for their Executive Command Center, which is fueled by the data we process
    ◦ 71% increase in sessions for users that were logging in due to increased data quality and reduced SLAs
Brandon was not subtle about giving credit to this team and was open about how much he enjoys working with Concord. These types of numbers are mind blowing and what keep us pushing our clients into the future :flying_saucer:

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

Everyone has been working very hard, and not without some stress and late nights. But, over the course of the last year, we've been able to make serious progress towards the lofty goals that were set before us.

Q4 earnings report came out this morning and we are continuing to see how our work is directly impacting their financial success. Q4 (calendar Q3) Sally Beauty eComm sales are up 23% and their B2B brand eComm sales are up 8%. There were two quotes  the CEO that I think sum up the amazing work that this team has been putting in. She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!” 
• :handshake: Google sellers specifically request Concord to support their clients based on past experiences working with our team. 
• :construction_worker: Concord has become the go-to partner for advertisers who’ve tried to go it alone or have worked with other partners without success. This team has proven time and again that we can get it done.

To give someone a taco, add a taco emoji after your message like this: `@username your positive attitude was a real help today! :taco:`  —
• If you tag *one person* and include *two tacos*, that person will receive *two tacos*.
• if you tag *two people* and include *one taco*, both people receive 1 taco.
• if you tag *two people* and include *two tacos*, both people receive 2 taco.
• If you tag *three people* and include *one taco*, each person will receive 1 taco.

She first said, unprompted:

• _I think if we step back, we just feel really good about the momentum in the business with the strategic initiatives driving over 250 basis points of comp this past year and those continuing into the new year. Focused on innovation, performance marketing, personalization, licensed colorist on demand._
Then, in response to this question  an investor: _Can you talk about the headwinds that you're seeing in Q1 and then how you plan to build over the course of the year to give you the confidence despite the volatile backdrop?_ The CEO responded:

• _I think first and foremost, when we think about Q1, I want to reemphasize we feel great about the underlying momentum in the business. What we've seen in terms of licensed colorist on demand, innovation, performance marketing, personalization, we think are all very strong... In terms of pacing and how we see the progression of impact, right, I think what's important to think about is as we go into 2027 and 2028, what customer activation can do for us and this understanding of the customer, being able to respond to that, incorporating how artificial intelligence can help us on that curve. We think the impact in personalization, performance marketing, and our LCOD campaign, I can't overstate the opportunity that we see there in what we're working on and delivering._
This is a direct testament to the hard-work the folks on the Sally scope have been putting in. Congrats on another successful quarter!

For the Analytics Development team, the Google partnership is keeping us busy so far in 2026:

• Ongoing Enhanced Conversions (EC) and Google Tag Gateway (GTG) work, with anywhere  5 - 10 new projects coming in weekly (49 new projects in 2026; 295 total non-GIPs projects opened since May 2025).
• A new GCS Implementation Partners (GIPs) program kicked off in late-January, requiring a new outreach structure as we’re responsible not only for _doing_ the EC and GTG work, but leading the sales effort in partnership with Google sellers. The current beta period includes 49 advertisers. Quarterly lead lists are expected to include 100+ new advertisers.
• Expanding EC and GTG work to include Google’s EMEA advertisers has begun. We’re expecting 20 new projects in the coming days, with the first 7 having come in just this morning.
• We learned this week that 2025's Shopify program is renewing for 2026. Last year we managed ~900 individual projects (completing approximately 35%, exceeding targets) for Shopify Plus customers. For 2026 we’ll target non-Plus advertisers. We expect less volume (more customers, but fewer will qualify for Google’s program due to smaller ad spend) but even 50% is 400+ individual projects in Q2-Q3.
Managing all of this is not an easy task. Keeping up with and completing all of the work is an even larger task. Through it all, however, I can’t say enough about how this team shines!

• :first_place_medal: A Google team member told us yesterday that “out of all the partners, Concord is the golden partner. Everyone wants to work with you!” 
• :handshake: Google sellers specifically request Concord to support their clients based on past experiences working with our team. 
• :construction_worker: Concord has become the go-to partner for advertisers who’ve tried to go it alone or have worked with other partners without success. This team has proven time and again that we can get it done.

I’ve long said that only a small portion of what we do as consultants is the actual work. I believe that one of the larger responsibilities we take on is in building and maintaining relationships. It is in building trust, earning respect, and making our stakeholders shine in their roles. If we fail to honor these responsibilities, it doesn’t matter how smart we are or good we are at solving problems. Our abilities get us in the door. Being good humans keeps us in the conversation.

With this in mind, I’m offering my last :taco: for today to @Chase Porter. Hello all, we copied all the spaces that were marked with "Vladimir" in the table. They are marked in green, have the date we moved them and in the last field there is a link to Concord Confluence.

Basic permissions like groups and users we have fixed them. It remains for us to go through the internally locked pages and fix the group policies, because they are not transferred during transfer, but only to a specific user. We'll fix them tomorrow, but you might want to look at the transferred spaces.

 It turned out that everything is transferred to 100% and not as I thought only the unlocked pages, they just become visible as they are in the Authentic part after the internally locked group policies are fixed. This is good information for us because we now have complete clarity on transferring the confluence spaces and permissions and there will be no problem transferring the active client spaces.

 If they are linked to a Jira project they will need to be transferred after the Jira project transfer to preserve all links. For those that we have transferred now, after we transfer Jira we will go through the projects and manually fix the shortcut between confluence and Jira.

 If you can think of anything else, we're here.

Postscript: in the table, we have not updated the fields on sheets 2 and 3, which spaces have been migrated, etc. You can update them according to your process and how you manage it.

Desiree wanted us to set a time to transfer Space to come up with a plan, but it's different for everyone. Up to 2 GB spaces are transferred in about 30 minutes. Above this size between 1 and 3 hours per space.
